rationale: In the citing paper, Bradlow and Rao (2000) is cited once in a grouped literature-review sentence as an example of prior multicategory-choice research on assortments. The citation mainly helps position the domain contrast (perfect compatibility in prior work versus incompatibility in this paper), rather than informing the paper's specific hypotheses, model structure, estimation, or interpretation. There is no distinct follow-on use of that paper's hierarchical Bayes assortment framework in the citing paper's core development.
